page: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processors. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in memory and compute-intensive tasks. The instance achieves strong results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 63240.19. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong at 3571.17 GB/sec, though large block read and write speeds fall into the average tier. The CPU features advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, which contribute to top-tier text generation speeds of 351.99 tokens/sec for small LLM models and strong prompt processing across small, medium, and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.12xlarge offers high resource density with its DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon cores, balancing compute power and memory capacity without the premium cost of specialized GPU or local NVMe storage hardware. However, the lack of local SSDs and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, limiting its suitability for workloads requiring ultra-low latency local storage or heavy parallel graphics processing. This instance is best suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 192.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows strong performance in single-core and multi-core operations, with high marks in Passmark CPU tests and Redis SET operations. The inclusion of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions enables top-tier performance in small LLM text generation and strong prompt processing. This resource profile offers balanced cost efficiency for enterprise applications, making it highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering strong database and CPU-based inference performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.12xlarge features 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR5 memory, and advanced matrix extensions for CPU-based workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores of dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processors.
Equipped with 192.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 4.0 GB per core.
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix math.
Delivers strong performance in database operations, Redis SET, and static web serving.
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached resources.
Optimized for enterprise applications, database workloads, and CPU-based LLM inference.
